content.problemStatement:
Problem StatementLittle Chef doesn't love math anymore. He loves Aleksandra.


 
Sashen'ka is nice. They spend all the time together. Even their birthdays they are celebrating together, because they were born on the same day :)



And now, this day is coming...



Chef know that most of all Sasha loves numbers, that's why he bought **N** positive numbers to a girl.
From her side, Sasha as all girls, thought that if she likes something, then all people like that too. And bought to a boy **N** numbers too. xD
After all greeting young people decide to play a game with their gifts. The game will be continued for **N **turns, in each turn the following will be done:


**x****y****xy > yx****x,y**
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single integer N on the first line, followed by two lines containing N space-separated integers each.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the probability that the girl will kiss the boy as a floating point number.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Example case 1. Game will have only 1 turn. Chef will choose 3, while Sasha will choose 4. Since 34>43 girl will kiss boy. Hence answer is 1.

Example case 2. Game will have 2 turns. In the first turn, Chef will choose 2 and Sasha will choose 4. Since 24>42 girl will kiss boy. In the second turn, Chef will choose 1 and Sasha will choose 3. Since 13>31 girl will kiss boy. Hence answer is 1.

Example case 3. Game will have 3 turns. In the first turn, Chef will choose 3 and Sasha will choose 6. Since 36<63 girl will not kiss boy. In the second turn, Chef will choose 2 and Sasha will choose 5. Since 25<52 girl will not kiss boy. In the third turn, Chef will choose 1 and Sasha will choose 4. Since 14<41 girl will not kiss boy. Hence answer is 0.

solution.approach: The key insight is to compare the concatenated numbers from the largest to the smallest. We use a simple comparison to determine if the girl will kiss the boy in each turn.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case due to sorting, O(T * N log N) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for storing the input arrays.

code.Python3:
import sys

T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(input())
    A = list(map(int, input().split()))
    B = list(map(int, input().split()))
    A.sort()
    B.sort()
    count = 0
    for i in range(N):
        str1 = str(A[N-1-i]) + str(B[N-1-i])
        str2 = str(B[N-1-i]) + str(A[N-1-i])
        if int(str1) > int(str2):
            count += 1
    print(count / N)
